args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json

# Load article data
articles_file = var_functions.query_db:2
if isinstance(articles_file, str) and articles_file.endswith('.json'):
    with open(articles_file, 'r') as f:
        articles = json.load(f)
else:
    articles = articles_file

# Load metadata
metadata_file = var_functions.query_db:6
if isinstance(metadata_file, str) and metadata_file.endswith('.json'):
    with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
        metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    metadata = metadata_file

# Convert to DataFrames for easier manipulation
df_articles = pd.DataFrame(articles)
df_metadata = pd.DataFrame(metadata)

# Display basic info about the datasets
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'articles_shape': list(df_articles.shape),
    'metadata_shape': list(df_metadata.shape),
    'sample_articles': df_articles.head(3).to_dict(orient='records'),
    'sample_metadata': df_metadata.head(3).to_dict(orient='records')
}))

var_functions.query_db:2.title: Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:2.description: Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again.

var_functions.query_db:2.title: Carlyle Looks Toward Commercial Aerospace (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:2.description: Reuters - Private investment firm Carlyle Group,\which has a reputation for making well-timed and occasionally\controversial plays in the defense industry, has quietly placed\its bets on another part of the market.

var_functions.query_db:2.title: Oil and Economy Cloud Stocks' Outlook (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:2.description: Reuters - Soaring crude prices plus worries\about the economy and the outlook for earnings are expected to\hang over the stock market next week during the depth of the\summer doldrums.

var_functions.query_db:2.title: Iraq Halts Oil Exports from Main Southern Pipeline (Reuters)
var_functions.query_db:2.description: Reuters - Authorities have halted oil export\flows from the main pipeline in southern Iraq after\intelligence showed a rebel militia could strike\infrastructure, an oil official said on Saturday.

var_functions.query_db:2.title: Oil prices soar to all-time record, posing new menace to US economy (AFP)
var_functions.query_db:2.description: AFP - Tearaway world oil prices, toppling records and straining wallets, present a new economic menace barely three months before the US presidential elections.

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json

# Load article data - get the variable from locals()
articles_key = 'var_functions.list_db:0'  # Actually this is just the collection name, need the actual query result
articles_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:2']

# Load metadata
metadata_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']

# Load the actual data if they are file paths
if isinstance(articles_data, str) and articles_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(articles_data, 'r') as f:
        articles = json.load(f)
else:
    articles = articles_data

if isinstance(metadata_data, str) and metadata_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(metadata_data, 'r') as f:
        metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    metadata = metadata_data

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'num_articles': len(articles),
    'num_metadata': len(metadata),
    'sample_article': articles[0] if articles else None,
    'sample_metadata': metadata[0] if metadata else None
}))

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json
import re

# Load articles data
articles_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:12']
if isinstance(articles_data, str) and articles_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(articles_data, 'r') as f:
        articles = json.load(f)
else:
    articles = articles_data

# Load metadata
metadata_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
if isinstance(metadata_data, str) and metadata_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(metadata_data, 'r') as f:
        metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    metadata = metadata_data

# Convert to DataFrames
articles_df = pd.DataFrame(articles)
metadata_df = pd.DataFrame(metadata)

# Check data info
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'articles_count': len(articles_df),
    'metadata_count': len(metadata_df),
    'articles_columns': articles_df.columns.tolist(),
    'metadata_columns': metadata_df.columns.tolist(),
    'sample_articles': articles_df.head(2).to_dict('records'),
    'sample_metadata': metadata_df.head(2).to_dict('records')
}))

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json
import re
from datetime import datetime

# Load articles data
articles_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:12']
if isinstance(articles_data, str) and articles_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(articles_data, 'r') as f:
        articles = json.load(f)
else:
    articles = articles_data

# Load metadata
metadata_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
if isinstance(metadata_data, str) and metadata_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(metadata_data, 'r') as f:
        metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    metadata = metadata_data

# Convert to DataFrames
articles_df = pd.DataFrame(articles)
metadata_df = pd.DataFrame(metadata)

# Convert data types
articles_df['article_id'] = articles_df['article_id'].astype(str)
metadata_df['article_id'] = metadata_df['article_id'].astype(str)

# Merge the data
merged_df = pd.merge(articles_df, metadata_df, on='article_id', how='inner')

# Convert publication_date to datetime
merged_df['publication_date'] = pd.to_datetime(merged_df['publication_date'])
merged_df['year'] = merged_df['publication_date'].dt.year

# Filter for Europe and years 2010-2020
europe_articles = merged_df[
    (merged_df['region'] == 'Europe') & 
    (merged_df['year'] >= 2010) & 
    (merged_df['year'] <= 2020)
]

# Display initial stats
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'merged_count': len(merged_df),
    'europe_articles_count': len(europe_articles),
    'europe_years_range': f"{europe_articles['year'].min()}-{europe_articles['year'].max()}",
    'years_in_range': len(europe_articles['year'].unique()),
    'sample_europe': europe_articles.head(3).to_dict('records')
}))

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json
import re
from datetime import datetime

# Load articles data
articles_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:12']
if isinstance(articles_data, str) and articles_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(articles_data, 'r') as f:
        articles = json.load(f)
else:
    articles = articles_data

# Load metadata
metadata_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:6']
if isinstance(metadata_data, str) and metadata_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(metadata_data, 'r') as f:
        metadata = json.load(f)
else:
    metadata = metadata_data

# Convert to DataFrames
articles_df = pd.DataFrame(articles)
metadata_df = pd.DataFrame(metadata)

# Convert data types
articles_df['article_id'] = articles_df['article_id'].astype(str)
metadata_df['article_id'] = metadata_df['article_id'].astype(str)

# Merge the data
merged_df = pd.merge(articles_df, metadata_df, on='article_id', how='inner')

# Convert publication_date to datetime
merged_df['publication_date'] = pd.to_datetime(merged_df['publication_date'])
merged_df['year'] = merged_df['publication_date'].dt.year

# Filter for Europe and years 2010-2020
europe_articles = merged_df[
    (merged_df['region'] == 'Europe') & 
    (merged_df['year'] >= 2010) & 
    (merged_df['year'] <= 2020)
]

# Create a simple function to classify business articles - basic keyword approach
business_keywords = ['stock', 'stocks', 'wall st', 'wall street', 'economy', 'oil', 'trade', 'google', 'hp', 'interest rates', 'dollar', 'deficit', 'money', 'shares', 'profits', 'business', 'market', 'investment', 'finance', 'financial', 'company', 'companies', 'bank', 'banking', 'export', 'import', 'auction'] 

def is_business_article(title, description):
    text = (title + ' ' + description).lower()
    for keyword in business_keywords:
        if keyword in text:
            return True
    return False

# Classify articles
europe_articles['is_business'] = europe_articles.apply(
    lambda row: is_business_article(row['title'], row['description']), axis=1
)

# Count business articles by year
business_by_year = europe_articles[europe_articles['is_business']].groupby('year').size()

# Create results dict
result_dict = {
    'total_europe_articles': len(europe_articles),
    'total_business_articles': europe_articles['is_business'].sum(),
    'business_by_year': {str(year): int(count) for year, count in business_by_year.items()},
    'sample_business_titles': europe_articles[europe_articles['is_business']].head(5)['title'].tolist()
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result_dict))

# Merge the data
merged_df = pd.merge(articles_df, metadata_df, on='article_id', how='inner')

# Convert publication_date to datetime
merged_df['publication_date'] = pd.to_datetime(merged_df['publication_date'])
merged_df['year'] = merged_df['publication_date'].dt.year

# Filter for Europe and years 2010-2020
europe_articles = merged_df[
    (merged_df['region'] == 'Europe') & 
    (merged_df['year'] >= 2010) & 
    (merged_df['year'] <= 2020)
]

# Create a simple function to classify business articles - basic keyword approach
business_keywords = ['stock', 'stocks', 'wall st', 'wall street', 'economy', 'oil', 'trade', 'google', 'hp', 'interest rates', 'dollar', 'deficit', 'money', 'shares', 'profits', 'business', 'market', 'investment', 'finance', 'financial', 'company', 'companies', 'bank', 'banking', 'export', 'import', 'auction'] 

def is_business_article(title, description):
    text = (title + ' ' + description).lower()
    for keyword in business_keywords:
        if keyword in text:
            return True
    return False

# Classify articles
europe_articles['is_business'] = europe_articles.apply(
    lambda row: is_business_article(row['title'], row['description']), axis=1
)

# Count business articles by year
business_by_year = europe_articles[europe_articles['is_business']].groupby('year').size()

# Create results dict
result_dict = {
    'total_europe_articles': len(europe_articles),
    'total_business_articles': europe_articles['is_business'].sum(),
    'business_by_year': {str(year): int(count) for year, count in business_by_year.items()},
    'sample_business_titles': europe_articles[europe_articles['is_business']].head(5)['title'].tolist()
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result_dict))
